document.masterDocTitle: Revision of the genus Georissus (Coleoptera, Hydrophiloidea, Georissidae) of Japan

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Revision of the genus Georissus (Coleoptera, Hydrophiloidea, Georissidae) of Japan

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Entomological Laboratory, Faculty of Agriculture, Ehime University, Tarumi 3 - 5 - 7, Matsuyama, 790 - 8566 Japan.
Entomological Laboratory, Faculty of Agriculture, Ehime University, Tarumi 3 - 5 - 7, Matsuyama, 790 - 8566 Japan.

bibRefCitation.author: Sharp D.
bibRefCitation.refString: Sharp D. 1888. Descriptions of some new Coleoptera from Japan. The Annals and Magazine of Natural History Series 6 2: 242 - 245. https: // doi. org / 10.1080 / 00222938809460918

subSubSection.paragraph: Figs 2A , 4A, D, G, J, M , 5A , 6A , 7A , 8A , 9A , 10A , 12A ; Table 1

This species resembles palearctic species
G . crenulatus
, and is distinguished from the latter by the presence of shallow longitudinal groove on posterior portion of pronotum (lacking in
G . crenulatus
).

MALE GENITALIA ( Fig. 9A ). Aedeagus 0.40 mm long. Parameres slightly shorter than phallobase; basal portions combined as wide as anterior portion of phallobase; lateral margins slightly bulbous at anterior portion; internal margins asymmetrical; anterior portion with short setae densely. Median lobe shorter than parameres, gradually narrowing apically; basal struts short. Phallobase about 1.8 times as long as wide, widening posteriorly; posterior portion with large basal foramen.
This species is common in Hokkaido and inhabits wet sandy river beds in summer ( Sharp 1888 ; Miwa 1937 ).
